item.title: The Oura Ring 5 Makes a Strong Case to First-Time Buyers
item.description: People who already own one of the company’s older models have less of a reason to upgrade to the $399 device.

item.title: Russia Sells Stake in Seized Top Gold Miner for $1.3 Billion
item.description: Russia sold a top gold miner that it seized from a billionaire last year, though only after multiple attempts and for about half the price it originally sought.

item.title: India Rate Setters See Outlook Improve as Middle East Risks Ease
item.description: India’s rate setters chose to stay growth-supportive and hold off on any policy pivot, viewing Middle East uncertainty as a reason for caution while expecting the economic outlook to improve rapidly once tensions ease.

item.title: ‘Take Risk’: Canada Regulator Cuts Bank Capital Level to Boost Lending
item.description: Canada’s financial regulator lowered capital requirements for the country’s largest banks for the first time in three years, giving them flexibility to lend more to support a domestic push for defense spending, critical infrastructure and artificial intelligence.

item.title: Canada Retail Sales Up 1% in May as Gasoline Lifts Receipts
item.description: Canadian retail sales continue to rise in the second quarter, lifted by surging gasoline prices that are crowding out many other spending categories.

item.title: India Stock Regulator Lets Firms Buy Back Shares From Market
item.description: India’s securities regulator will reintroduce open-market buybacks for companies to support share prices in a market that has lagged global peers.

item.title: Vitol Won Emergency Zambia Fuel Supply Deal That IMF Questioned
item.description: Vitol Group is supplying diesel to Zambia via exclusive access to a pipeline that lasts through September, in an emergency arrangement the International Monetary Fund has urged authorities to end.

item.title: AutoForm’s Debt Rejig Highlights Revived Appetite for Software
item.description: Investors in European collateralized loan obligations piled into AutoForm’s latest deal, the first test of demand for junk-debt borrowers in the software sector since a selloff earlier this year.

item.title: Lutnick’s Anthropic Crackdown Claims New Power Over AI Models
item.description: The Trump administration’s push to rein in Anthropic PBC, outlined in a recent Commerce Department order, relies on an unprecedented use of export control laws and raises legal questions about whether the US can dictate who can access artificial intelligence systems.

item.title: Scatec Says Egypt Solar Project Can Save $400 Million Over LNG
item.description: Scatec ASA’s Obelisk solar and battery storage project in Egypt can save the country as much as $400 million a year in liquefied natural gas imports, according to Chief Executive Officer Terje Pilskog.

item.title: Ambani’s Jio Weighs India Satellite Network to Rival Starlink
item.description: Billionaire Mukesh Ambani-controlled Jio Platforms Ltd. is weighing plans to build its own satellite constellation, as it seeks to cement control over the nation’s communications infrastructure while Elon Musk’s Starlink faces hurdles.

item.title: Asian Oil Buyers Brace for Flood of Crude From Persian Gulf
item.description: The biggest customers for Middle Eastern supplies are under pressure to take cargoes or face penalties.

item.title: Chipmakers Renew Nerdy Performance Tussle That Nvidia’s Dominance Had Quashed
item.description: With CPUs back in the spotlight, so too is the PR fight over benchmarks

item.title: Turkish Fintech Firm Midas Plans Expansion Into Payment Systems
item.description: Turkish financial technologies firm Midas has applied to the central bank for an electronic money institution license as it seeks to launch digital consumer payments products.

item.title: Trump’s Iran Deal Includes a Mindful Eye on the Dollar’s Global Role
item.description: The dominance of the US dollar has seen some waning over the years.

item.title: Russia Surprises With Small Rate Cut Even as Inflation Eases
item.description: Russia’s central bank lowered borrowing costs less than expected, even as a stronger ruble spurred by the Middle East conflict helped slow inflation.

item.title: Czech Billionaire Premier Feuds With Central Bank Over Rate Hike
item.description: The Czech prime minister heightened his public criticism of the central bank’s tight monetary policy, saying its latest move to raise interest rates will hurt the economy.

item.title: The Dollar Is Riding High Thanks to a Newly Hawkish Fed
item.description: Greenback’s strength is weighing on emerging-market currencies

item.title: World Cup Mints Big Prediction Winners and Million-Dollar Losers
item.description: The World Cup is turning prediction markets into a public ledger of million-dollar bets, exposing big wins and dramatic losses as the tournament attracts more money to the fast-growing industry.

item.title: Santander Surpasses Inditex as Spain’s Most Valuable Company
item.description: Banco Santander SA has become Spain’s most valuable listed company, overtaking Zara owner Inditex SA for the first time in eight years.

item.title: Indian Banks Line Up $2.5 Billion Debt Sales Using RBI Swap
item.description: At least four lenders including the State Bank of India are preparing to raise about $2.5 billion selling bonds in the coming weeks, tapping a Reserve Bank of India facility that makes it cheaper to borrow dollars, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US Stocks Draw Record Weekly Inflows as Investors Pile Into Tech
item.description: US stocks attracted record inflows last week as investors piled into the technology sector, according to Bank of America Corp. strategists.

item.title: Collapsed Lender MFS Said to Have Never Registered £300 Million of Mortgages
item.description: A £300 million ($396 million) portfolio of mortgages linked to collapsed lender Market Financial Solutions Ltd. looks to never have been registered with public property records, according to court filings, threatening to complicate lenders’ efforts to establish their claims over the underlying assets.

item.title: Europe Gas Storages Get Little Reprieve From Iran Peace Deal
item.description: European natural gas prices have mostly fallen since a US-Iran peace accord started to take shape this week, but the drop has done little to improve the economics of storing fuel for winter as analysts continue to expect a tight market.

item.title: South Africa Aims to Replicate Telkom’s Success With Property Firm
item.description: South Africa seeks to replicate the success achieved by bringing private partners into telecommunications firm Telkom SA SOC Ltd. with the creation of a new state company that will manage its 155-billion-rand ($9.4 billion) property portfolio.

item.title: Indian Billionaires Set for Jackpot on National Stock Exchange IPO
item.description: Some of India’s wealthiest investors stand to get even richer from their shareholdings in the nation’s stock exchange, which filed for an initial public offering this week.

item.title: Candidates Deploy Army of Poll Watchers in Colombia’s High-Stakes Election
item.description: Colombia’s presidential campaigns are both flagging the risk of fraud in Sunday’s election, and taking steps to prevent it.

item.title: Mukesh Ambani Says Jio Platforms to File IPO Papers Friday
item.description: Billionaire Mukesh Ambani said Reliance Industries Ltd.’s digital unit will submit its listing documents by Friday, starting the process of unlocking shareholder value in one of India’s most-anticipated initial public offerings.

item.title: Siemens Sees Industrial App Store Sales Doubling in Tech Push
item.description: Siemens AG expects revenue from its online store to more than double this year, though the maker of industrial software and trains is stopping short of disclosing concrete figures that would track its progress of turning into a tech-led company.

item.title: Iran War Fuels Interest in Renewables, Multilateral Lender Says
item.description: The conflict in Iran, which has sent crude prices to multi-year highs, is fueling interest in renewables and projects aimed at boosting energy security, according to the Private Infrastructure Development Group.
